summary: This bundle translates a previously delivered blueprint and patch doctrine into literal code stubs for a MuranoOS-native Phase I admitted room shell — the Western Myth Codex.
final_use_case: It preserves the distinctive design logic for Western Myth Codex, so the portfolio can show the actual mechanism and status instead of reducing the project to a file-count or handoff label.

instruments.input: source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.operation: This bundle translates a previously delivered blueprint and patch doctrine into literal code stubs for a MuranoOS-native Phase I admitted room shell — the Western Myth Codex.
instruments.output: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime and produce bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct.
instruments.verification_check: Open or inspect the local runtime path for Western Myth Codex Handoff Identity; confirm a room, action, or state record traces to the governing contract.

instruments.instrument_name: Western Myth Codex Room Task Model

instruments.instrument_name: Western Myth Codex Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library.

instruments.input: frontend, backend, storage, retrieval, auth, export, and portfolio linkage
instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Add the five new files under frontend/src/rooms/myth-codex/ and src-tauri/src/. 2. Apply patches/frontend_admission_snippets.md to governed routing, route registry, room contracts, cross-domain sidebar. 3. Apply patches/backend_registration_snippets.md to db.rs (run schema + seed) and main.rs (register the 9 Tauri commands). 4. Launch MuranoOS, confirm /myth-codex admits the room shell, that health check returns.
instruments.output: build sequence for turning the handoff into working software

instruments.instrument_name: Western Myth Codex / MuranoOS-native Phase I
instruments.input: project source text and the named component `MuranoOS-native Phase I`
instruments.operation: uses `MuranoOS-native Phase I` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: This bundle translates a previously delivered blueprint and patch doctrine into literal code stubs for a MuranoOS-native Phase I admitted room shell — the Western Myth Codex. The room's mission is to catalog, compare, and interpret a 1000-item Western myth corpus without collapsing three distinct state concerns: live scholarly analysis, archive state, and handoff continuity.
